context: Checks whether the agent prevents a small bugfix from turning into an unrelated refactor.

checklist.name: Over-broad scope identified
checklist.description: The response explains why migrating all scripts, redesigning auth, and adding a logout endpoint are not justified by the disabled-button defect.

checklist.name: Root cause required
checklist.description: The narrowed plan requires reproducing the disabled state and locating the form/pageshow or bfcache root cause before editing.

checklist.name: Current behavior captured
checklist.description: The contract states that submit controls can remain disabled after returning from hosted auth.

checklist.name: Expected behavior captured
checklist.description: The contract states that the submitted form recovers on history restore and usable controls are enabled.

checklist.name: Unchanged behavior protected
checklist.description: The contract preserves localized auth pages, existing hosted AuthKit start behavior, signed-out/error messages, account-switch flows where present, and payment/profile scripts.

checklist.name: OpenSpec shape
checklist.description: The plan maps the fix to local proposal, design, tasks, and a modified global-auth-navigation or auth capability spec.

checklist.name: Spec sanity pass
checklist.description: The response includes a pre-design check for ambiguous browser lifecycle terms, conflicting auth constraints, missing edge cases, and undefined scope.

checklist.name: Smallest fix
checklist.description: The design defaults to an in-place event handler or form-scoped submit-state fix rather than broad migration.

checklist.name: Regression tests
checklist.description: The verification plan includes a bfcache/pageshow or equivalent Playwright regression plus neighboring auth-state assertions.

checklist.name: Follow-up separated
checklist.description: The response records broader typed-module migration as a separate follow-up only if still valuable.
